frameworks.name: decision variable encoding in parietal cortex
frameworks.one_line: LIP firing rates represent the accumulated decision variable for saccade-reported decisions

frameworks.name: competing-ensembles action-selection circuit
frameworks.one_line: Action-encoding ensembles compete with higher rates for stronger preferred-target evidence

frameworks.name: population state-space manifold
frameworks.one_line: DV is encoded along a curved population manifold that rotates and shifts by task

core_question: Do parietal decision-variable representations generalize across perceptual tasks reported via the same saccadic actions?
key_assumptions: Decisions are reported through saccadic eye movements
DV can be inferred from neural responses to graded sensory evidence
Same readout applies when actions are matched across tasks
manipulations: Task type: random-dot motion vs novel face discrimination
Stimulus strength supporting the preferred saccade target
Recording across LIP, lateral frontal, and medial frontal regions
key_findings: In face task, average LIP firing rates were lower for stronger preferred-target evidence
This contradicted the action-competition prediction of monotonic rate increase
Population responses in both tasks monotonically encoded the DV on a curved manifold
The curved manifold rotated and shifted in a task-dependent manner across LIP and frontal cortex
interpretations: Apparent average-rate differences reflect task-dependent geometry, not different computations
DV integration generalizes across tasks at the population manifold level
